navSections.title: High Level Design
navSections.blurb: Focuses on system architecture, components, and their interactions.

navSections.title: System Architectural Styles
navSections.blurb: Common architectural patterns used in system design.

navSections.title: Scalability
navSections.blurb: Concepts and strategies for growing applications.

items.label: Right Scalability approach for our Application
Primary Bottlenecks that Hurt the Scalability of an Application

navSections.title: Databases in Designing Systems
navSections.blurb: Databases, storage systems, and how they are used in system design.

items.label: Choosing a Database - SQL or NoSQL

navSections.title: Consistency, Availability, Reliability & Maintainability
navSections.blurb: Core system qualities that impact user experience and system health.

navSections.title: Load Balancing
navSections.blurb: Traffic distribution across instances and regions. CPU threads, goroutines, and in-process parallelism belong under Concurrency.

navSections.title: Latency, Throughput and Caching
navSections.blurb: System-wide SLOs, edge caching, and pipeline throughput. Per-service worker saturation and lock contention are covered under Concurrency.

items.label: Cold and Warm Cache in System Design

navSections.title: Message Queues & Async Handoffs
navSections.blurb: Durable buffers and pub/sub between services — decoupling producers from consumers. HTTP ingress, gateways, and real-time client APIs live under API Design.

navSections.title: Protocols, CDN & Proxies
navSections.blurb: L3–L7 plumbing, DNS, and edge caching for systems. Client-facing long-poll, WebSocket, and gateway routing patterns are covered under API Design.

navSections.title: Event-Driven Architecture
navSections.blurb: Explains event-driven concepts, patterns, and comparisons commonly discussed in system design interviews.

items.label: Restore State in an Event-Based, Message-Driven Microservice Architecture on Failure Scenario
Event-Driven Architecture Patterns in Cloud Native Applications

navSections.title: Testing at System Scale
navSections.blurb: Validating a distributed system end-to-end. Unit and typical integration tests are general engineering; contract and consumer tests for HTTP APIs live under API Design.

navSections.title: Security Measures
navSections.blurb: Platform-wide posture: encryption in transit, SDLC, and recovery. API keys, JWT, OAuth, and request authorization models are under API Design.

items.label: Secure Socket Layer (SSL) and Transport Layer Security (TLS)

navSections.title: Distributed System Design
navSections.blurb: Explains consensus algorithms, tracing, and security considerations in distributed system design.

navSections.title: Cost & Performance Optimizations
navSections.blurb: Explains how to optimize system performance and estimate software costs effectively.

concepts.title: Caching
concepts.summary: Speed up data access by storing frequently-read data in a fast-access layer. Explore LRU, LFU, and write-through strategies.

concepts.title: Load Balancing
concepts.summary: Distribute traffic across multiple servers using round-robin, least-connections, and consistent hashing strategies.

concepts.title: Pub/Sub Messaging
concepts.summary: Decouple services using publish-subscribe patterns to enable scalable asynchronous communication.

concepts.title: Database Sharding
concepts.summary: Scale databases horizontally by partitioning data across multiple nodes using range or hash-based strategies.

concepts.title: Message Queues
concepts.summary: Buffer and process workloads asynchronously with durable queues to smooth traffic spikes and decouple producers from consumers.
